policy.source: manual instruction-first visual inspection plus RestaurantDB tool reasoning
policy.notes: Only cases in this file are considered manually rebuilt. Other tasks remain pending until inspected.

cases.difference_summary: Official GT likely selects Salmon & asparagus, but Italian Pasta savory lowest sodium by DB is Pesto. The official analysis field also conflicts with the instruction for the second add.
Manual visual and DB review supports the official GT.
Official GT uses Culatello, but if first pointed section is Cold Cuts and the instruction asks lowest price after discount among high-sodium dishes, Salame Milano has the lower discounted price.

The visual category below Selected Steaks appears to be the Handmade Bread section, not Cheese & Olives. Since the found bread price is not above 80, only the final highest-calorie bread should be added.
Manual visual review maps the top left fold to Cold Cuts, the top middle fold to Antipasti & Snacks, and the top right fold to Pizza. DB filtering supports the official additions.

The first pointed category is Cold Cuts and the second pointed category is Pizza. Cold Cuts has no vegan dish, so the fallback branch through Sandwiches & Panini is correct; the Pizza dairy lowest discounted-price selection is Margherita.
The dark-background right-fold section is Selected Steaks and contains no Italian Classic Set dish. The fallback and subsequent Annie's top dishes high-fiber selection match the DB.

The top right fold is Pizza and includes non-discounted dishes. The section to the right of the initial Cold Cuts point resolves to Antipasti & Snacks, and DB filtering supports the official additions.
The lower-middle middle-fold section is Italian Pasta and has no egg allergen dishes. The fallback Cold Cuts and Salads selections match DB nutrition/taste filters.
The right-fold left-border hand section is Salads and contains no Annie's Special Set dish. The fallback Pizza selection and Sandwiches & Panini low-fat selection match DB fields.

The bottom-right bread section average tax rate is below 0.1. DB nutrition supports Wagyu & veggies skewers and Chicken wings as the high-protein lowest-fat Antipasti choices, then Annie's pizza as the high-sodium largest-discount Annie's top dish.
The top right fold is Pizza and contains spicy dishes. The Sandwiches & Panini low-fat lowest-price candidate and second-pointed Pizza cheapest candidate match the official trace.
The top middle panel is Antipasti & Snacks and contains rich-flavored dishes. Selected Steaks and Handmade Bread DB filters support the official additions.
The top left category is Cold Cuts and includes salty/slightly salty dishes. The Salads high-iron lowest-sodium and Antipasti high-fiber lowest-calorie selections match the DB.
With the center-left category resolved as Cheese & Olives, the all-tax-equals-0.05 condition is false. The visually referenced Salads and Sandwiches & Panini follow-up selections match the DB.

The top middle fold is Antipasti & Snacks and not all items contain gluten, so the fallback to last-pointed Italian Pasta is correct. The top-left Cold Cuts low-fat discounted-price selection also matches DB.
The bottom-left small card is Annie's top dishes and contains Seafood Delight Set dishes. Cold Cuts spicy highest-protein and Pizza creamy largest-discount candidates match the official trace.
The top middle fold is Antipasti & Snacks and not all dishes contain dairy. The fallback Italian Pasta spicy highest-calorie and Cheese & Olives high-fiber highest-protein selections match DB.
The right-fold left-border section is Salads and has dishes above 60 yuan. The second-pointed Pizza and middle-fold right-border Sandwiches & Panini selections match the official DB trace.
The top right fold is Pizza and has no Steak Lovers Set dish. The fallback last-pointed Italian Pasta spicy candidate is Rigatoni all'arrabbiata, and the allergen check does not trigger replacement.
All Handmade Bread items contain gluten. The last-pointed Italian Pasta low-fat lowest-calorie item is Aglio, olio & peperoncino; its spicy taste triggers replacement by Ham & mushroom from Pizza.
The lower-middle center panel is Italian Pasta and includes herbal Pesto. The DB set-meal, allergen, and Selected Steaks replacement checks support the official trace.
The top-left card is Cold Cuts and all listed dishes are discounted. The second-pointed Pizza high-sodium highest-price candidate is Parma ham, and no replacement is required.
The right-panel left-border section is Salads and contains fish-allergen dishes. The first-pointed Cold Cuts and top-middle Antipasti selections match DB filters.

The top right panel is Pizza and contains no Cold Cuts & Cheese Platter set dish. The middle-panel right-side hand section resolves to Sandwiches & Panini, and the last-pointed Italian Pasta gluten-free step yields no additional dish.

The lower-middle middle fold is Italian Pasta, so the all-tax-greater-than-0.08 condition is false. Using numeric nutrition/taste matching, the Selected Steaks and Pizza follow-up choices align with official GT.

Instruction asks for smoked/salty dishes priced 600-700 in Selected Steaks, but DB taste profiles for the Selected Steaks candidates are savory/rich or savory/buttery, not smoked/salty. Official GT also follows an analysis field that changes the instruction wording.

The official key/value identifies the bottom-left panel as Annie's top dishes, whose discounts are not lower than 0.8. Therefore the fallback branch should select Veggie salad from Salads, not Turkey breast ham from Cold Cuts.

Instruction asks for a high-protein lowest-price dish in the third pointed category, which supports Bolognese. The later Sandwiches & Panini condition says salty and umami, but DB has no sandwich with both taste labels; official GT adds Tuna panini after the analysis rewrites the condition as salty-savory/umami.
Manual visual entity grounding and DB/tool-condition review support the official GT.

Instruction asks to calculate total tax, but official GT calls compute_total_payment. The visual/DB branch and added dish are otherwise supported.

Salmone affumicato discount is 0.8, so the true branch is entered, but DB has no low-sugar Dessert item. Official GT instead adds a Pasta item and removes Margherita, which is not supported by the instruction branch.
Manual visual entity grounding and DB/tool-condition review support the official GT.

Official GT has a user_id typo (`ustomer_004`) in compute_total_tax; instruction and DB otherwise support the same dish addition.
Salmone affumicato sodium is exactly 800mg, so it does not exceed 800mg. The fallback rich-flavor Annie top dish is Lasagne, not Mushroom soup.

Official GT contains `Homemade pappardelle with bolognese sauce*`, which is not a DB dish name. The corrected trace removes the stray asterisk; quantity-halving semantics still need human review.
Official GT adds Walnut bread after clearing the order but computes tax for `Handmade Bread`, which is a category name rather than a DB dish.
